data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Kinder Morgan beats on topline, ends Q1 with backlog of $8.8B
RE/MAX says March sales increased 23% M/M
data.description:
• RE/MAX (RMAX) said March sales increased 23% over February, marking the largest month-over-month sales increase since 37.4% in March 2023. Compared to March 2024, however, sales were down 1.4%.
  • The surge in month-over-month sales across the 50 metro areas surveyed may have been fueled by a growing number of sellers and available properties.
  • The number of homes for sale in March increased 8.0% from February and totaled 35.5% more than March 2024.
  • Boosting the inventory was a 29.8% increase in new listings compared to February – and a 7.9% increase compared to a year ago.
  • March's Median Sales Price increased to $435,000, which was nearly $8,000 (1.8%) higher than in February and $15,000 (3.5%) more than a year ago.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Banner GAAP EPS of $1.30 beats by $0.08, revenue of $160.2M beats by $0.53M
data.description:
• Banner press release (NASDAQ:BANR): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.30 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $160.2M (+10.8% Y/Y) beats by $0.53M.
    • Net interest income was $141.1 million in the first quarter of 2025, compared to $140.5 million in the preceding quarter and increased 6% from $133.0 million in the first quarter a year ago.
    • Mortgage banking operations revenue was $3.1 million for the first quarter of 2025, compared to $3.7 million in the preceding quarter and $2.3 million in the first quarter a year ago.
    • Return on average assets was 1.15% for both the current and preceding quarter and 0.97% in the first quarter a year ago.
    • Net loans receivable increased to $11.28 billion at March 31, 2025, compared to $11.20 billion at December 31, 2024, and increased 5% compared to $10.72 billion at March 31, 2024.
    • Non-performing assets were $42.7 million, or 0.26% of total assets, at March 31, 2025, compared to $39.6 million, or 0.24% of total assets, at December 31, 2024 and $29.9 million, or 0.19% of total assets, at March 31, 2024.
    • The allowance for credit losses - loans was $157.3 million, or 1.38% of total loans receivable, as of March 31, 2025, compared to $155.5 million, or 1.37% of total loans receivable, as of December 31, 2024 and $151.1 million, or 1.39% of total loans receivable, as of March 31, 2024.
    • Total deposits increased to $13.59 billion at March 31, 2025, compared to $13.51 billion at December 31, 2024, and increased 3% compared to $13.16 billion at March 31, 2024.
    • Core deposits represented 89% of total deposits at March 31, 2025.
    • Dividends paid to shareholders were $0.48 per share in the quarter ended March 31, 2025.
    • Common shareholders’ equity per share increased 3% to $53.16 at March 31, 2025, compared to $51.49 at the preceding quarter end, and increased 10% from $48.39 at March 31, 2024.
    • Tangible common shareholders’ equity per share* increased 4% to $42.27 at March 31, 2025, compared to $40.57 at the preceding quarter end, and increased 13% from $37.40 at March 31, 2024.

    *Non-GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) financial measure; See, “Additional Financial Information - Non-GAAP Financial Measures” on the final two pages of this press release for a reconciliation of non-GAAP financial measures.

    Income Statement Review

data.title: CVR Energy nearly completes maintenance at Coffeyville refinery - report
data.description:
CVR Energy's (NYSE:CVI) Coffeyville refinery in Kansas should soon complete planned turnaround work, Dow Jones reported late Tuesday, adding that the plant may have been working through some issues during the restart process.

The report said a source said late last week that operators were planning to bring the 136K bbl/day refinery back online by this Friday - although this could be delayed by restart issues - but another source said CVR Energy (NYSE:CVI) was selling Group 3 ULSD (X-grade), which could mean the refinery's distillation unit has returned to service.

CEO Dave Lamp said in CVR Energy's (CVI) Q4 earnings call that the company would extend maintenance work at the Coffeyville refinery by 10-15 days, and the turnaround was expected to be completed by end of March, following a January fire at the plant's naphtha hydrotreater.

CVR (CVI) also said it was planning to use the turnaround to finish tie-in work for the initial phase of a project to increase the refinery's distillate production by 1,500 bbl/day.

data.title: Dollar Tree CFO buys shares worth ~$1.24M
data.description:
Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) said in a filing on Wednesday that CFO Stewart Glendinning has acquired shares worth ~$1.24M in the retail discount stores company.

DLTR shares were +2.09% to $73.30 ahead of the closing bell.

Glendinning bought 4,000 shares of common stock for $72.5 per share, and 13,000 shares for a weighted average price per share of $72.8269.

The CFO owns 49,353 shares of direct ownership post the transaction.

data.title: OCC blends large, midsize and community bank oversight in organizational overhaul
data.description:
The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency is merging its supervision of large, midsize and community banks, aiming to bolster efficiency in regulatory oversight, it said in a statement released Wednesday.

Organizational changes will take effect starting June 2, 2025.

The combination of the two supervision functions will effectively create the Bank Supervision and Examination line of business, enabling the OCC to smoothly share expertise and resources to address bank-specific issues, it said.

Greg Coleman, senior deputy comptroller for Large Bank Supervision, will lead the Bank Supervision and Examination office. 

In addition, the Chief National Bank Examiner, to be led by Jay Gallagher, the senior deputy comptroller for Supervision Risk and Analysis office will be reinstated. That will include the divisions of Bank Supervision Policy as well as Supervision Risk and Analysis.

The banking regulator also noted that it is elevating its Information Technology and Security function to be led by a new senior deputy comptroller for ITS, who will serve as a member of the Executive Committee.

data.title: New US export requirements on Nvidia, AMD part of effort against China's AI ambitions: report
data.description:
The new export licensing requirement targeting Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) artificial intelligence chips are part of a larger effort to block China's AI ambitions, according to a report by The New York Times.

Nvidia and AMD shares had both tumbled nearly 10% by afternoon trading on Wednesday.

The Trump Administration is considering applying penalties to block DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK), a China-based AI company, from purchasing any U.S. technology, the report said. It is also considering blocking Americans' access to DeepSeek. 

What's more, the House Select Committee on the Chinese Communist Party, released a new investigative report today that finds DeepSeek operates on tens of thousands of Nvidia chips, including some that are subject to U.S. export controls.

"This report makes it clear: DeepSeek isn't just another AI app — it's a weapon in the Chinese Communist Party's arsenal, designed to spy on Americans, steal our technology, and subvert U.S. law," said Chairman John Moolenaar (R-MI). "We now know this tool exploited U.S. AI models and reportedly used advanced Nvidia chips that should never have ended up in CCP hands. That's why we're sending a letter to Nvidia to demand answers. American innovation should never be the engine of our adversaries' ambitions."

Nvidia said it follows the proper protocol laid out by the U.S. government regarding the export of its hardware.

"The U.S. government instructs American businesses on what they can sell and where—we follow the government’s directions to the letter," a Nvidia spokesperson told Seeking Alpha.  "NVIDIA protects and enhances national security by creating U.S. jobs and infrastructure, promoting U.S. technology leadership, bringing billions of dollars of tax revenue to the U.S. treasury, and alleviating the massive U.S. trade deficit."

"The technology industry supports America when it exports to well-known companies worldwide - if the government felt otherwise, it would instruct us. Our reported Singapore revenue indicates the billing address, often for subsidiaries of our U.S. customers," the spokesperson added. "The associated products are shipped to other locations, including the United States and Taiwan, not to China."
